summary: Adjusted the homepage banner styling to stop aggressive image cropping by switching the hero to an aspect-ratio-based, contained background treatment with a taller minimum height and stronger overlay/text contrast for readability.
work_completed: Updated `assets/main.css` so `.banner-panel` uses the banner image with `background-size: cover, contain` instead of cover-only cropping.
Added an aspect-ratio-driven banner layout with minimum heights to preserve substantially more of the full banner image across desktop and mobile widths.
Tightened banner text readability with stronger text shadowing, constrained text width, and mobile overlay adjustments.
Kept `index.html` unchanged because the existing text-first banner structure was sufficient for the presentation fix.

findings.message: No HTML changes were required; the crop fix was contained to `assets/main.css`.

`docs/design-docs/site-architecture.md` currently describes small-screen banner behavior in terms of image crop shifting, which should be updated to reflect the new contained/letterboxed presentation.
No automated tests were added or run because this task is a static visual CSS adjustment without an existing visual test harness.

follow_up_recommendations: Update `docs/design-docs/site-architecture.md` so the Banner Implementation section matches the new non-cropping hero behavior.
Perform a quick manual browser check on desktop and mobile widths during review to confirm the overlay remains readable with the real rendered image.
